name: From Baeza to the Montalvas walk — circular
name_original: De Baeza al paseo de las Montalvas — circular
region_intro: Jogging around Baeza offers a unique blend of historic charm and natural beauty. The region is characterized by vast olive groves in the Guadalquivir valley, providing scenic backdrops for many running routes. Runners can explore the well-preserved Renaissance architecture of the UNESCO World Heritage historic center, with ancient city walls forming part of popular paths. The distant silhouette of the Sierra de Cazorla adds a mountainous element to the surrounding landscape. Best jogging routes around Baeza The most popular jogging route is From Baeza to the Montalvas walk — circular, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes about 51 minutes to complete. This route offers views over the expansive olive groves. Another top favourite among local runners is Around Baeza — circular by the Alcázar de Baeza, a moderate 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This route navigates through the historic center, passing significant architectural landmarks. Local runners also love the Jaén Gate – Leocadio Marín Park loop from Baeza, an 8.2 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through the historic city and Leocadio Marín Park, often completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes. Jogging around Baeza is defined by its olive groves, historic city walls, and Renaissance architecture. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Baeza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 380 runners have used komoot to explore Baeza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Baeza?
A: Baeza offers a diverse selection of over 20 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These trails allow you to explore the region's unique blend of historic charm and natural beauty.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for running trails in Baeza?
A: You'll find a good mix of difficulty levels for running in Baeza. There are a few easy routes perfect for a gentle jog, around 16 moderate trails that offer a bit more challenge, and a few difficult options for experienced runners seeking a more strenuous workout.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while jogging in Baeza?
A: Jogging in Baeza immerses you in quintessential Andalusian landscapes. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367509305/from-baeza-to-the-montalvas-walk-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Baeza to the Montalvas walk — circular&lt;/a&gt;, offer breathtaking views of vast olive groves and the picturesque Guadalquivir valley. You can also admire the distant silhouette of the Sierra de Cazorla National Park, adding a dramatic mountainous backdrop to your run.

Q: Can I combine running with exploring Baeza's historic sites?
A: Absolutely! Baeza's UNESCO World Heritage historic center is integrated into many running paths. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367508522/around-baeza-circular-by-the-alcazar-de-baeza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Around Baeza — circular by the Alcázar de Baeza&lt;/a&gt; navigate through the town's Renaissance splendor, allowing you to pass significant landmarks such as the Plaza del Pópulo, the Cathedral, and the Palacio de Jabalquinto. The ancient city walls, particularly the 'Paseo de las Murallas', also form part of popular running paths.

Q: Are there any running routes with scenic viewpoints in Baeza?
A: Yes, several routes offer stunning panoramic views. The 'Paseo de Antonio Machado' (also known as 'The Walls' or 'Paseo de las Murallas') is renowned for its breathtaking outlooks over the Guadalquivir valley and olive groves, especially at sunset. The Mirador de las Murallas along this path provides particularly rewarding vistas.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Baeza?
A: Yes, Baeza has several excellent circular running routes. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367509305/from-baeza-to-the-montalvas-walk-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Baeza to the Montalvas walk — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views over expansive olive groves, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367508522/around-baeza-circular-by-the-alcazar-de-baeza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Around Baeza — circular by the Alcázar de Baeza&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the historic center.

Q: Are there options for longer distance running trails near Baeza?
A: For those looking for longer runs, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24524622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Barbican – Old University Church loop from Baeza&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 15.3 km route. Additionally, the Greenway Nature Trail of the Renaissance (Sierra de Alcaraz), accessible from the Baeza area, is a 77-kilometer former railway line offering varied landscapes for extended scenic runs.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Baeza?
A: The running trails in Baeza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 5 reviews. Runners frequently praise the unique combination of historic charm and natural beauty, highlighting the scenic olive groves and the opportunity to jog through the well-preserved Renaissance architecture of the city.

Q: Are there any parks or green spaces incorporated into running routes in Baeza?
A: Yes, some running loops in Baeza include green spaces. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaén Gate – Leocadio Marín Park loop from Baeza&lt;/a&gt; leads through the historic city and incorporates Leocadio Marín Park, providing a pleasant green environment within or on the immediate outskirts of the town.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I might see along the running routes?
A: As you run, you'll encounter numerous historical and natural points of interest. Within the city, you might pass the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Chapel of the Savior&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7245052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baeza Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;. Outside the city, the routes offer views towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4754151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to the Sierra Mágina Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, providing a rich cultural and scenic experience.

Q: Are there any specific routes that are particularly good for experiencing the olive groves?
A: To truly experience Baeza's iconic olive groves, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367509305/from-baeza-to-the-montalvas-walk-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Baeza to the Montalvas walk — circular&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This route offers expansive views over the olive-carpeted landscape, providing a quintessential Andalusian running experience.
tips: This square, known as Plaza del Pópulo, houses the Tourist Office, the fountain and the Jaén Gate, attached to part of the old city wall.
